title: Rust pure happy-path: committed Cargo.lock provisions and tests offline
description:
## Parent
dustcastle-gy5 тАФ Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)

## What to build
The core end-to-end tracer bullet: a Rust repo with a committed `Cargo.lock` is detected, provisioned pure into the shared Store, and runs `cargo test` fully offline in the Sandbox тАФ with zero `SandboxStaging` interface change.

Cuts through every layer: detection recognises `rust` / `cargo` from `Cargo.toml`+`Cargo.lock`; the new Rust Importer emits the spike-proven Nix shape; the store discovers the aggregate `cargoHash` via the existing fake-hash тЖТ mismatch тЖТ real-hash loop; env-only `cp -RL <deps> $CARGO_HOME` staging plus `CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true` runs the offline build/test; a pure build derives egress `none`.

Add `"rust"` to the `Ecosystem` union and `"cargo"` to the `PackageManager` union; register `RUST_ECOSYSTEM` (single Package Manager, no impurity signal, generic loose default, env-only staging) and the `cargo` descriptor (`generateBuild` тЖТ the Importer). Introduce the shared `CARGO_HOME` basename constant agreed between the Importer (emits config.toml) and the stager.

Prototype-derived Importer shape (from `spike-rust-cargo-fod/deps.nix`, GO verdict тАФ trimmed to the decisions, not a working demo):

```nix
# (1) FOD: network ON, ONE aggregate cargoHash (the store's two-pass discovery pins it).
cargoVendor = pkgs.rustPlatform.fetchCargoVendor { inherit src; name = ...; inherit hash; };

# Shared constant agreed with the stager. NOT a SandboxStaging change.
cargoHomeBasename = "dustcastle-cargo-home";

# (2) PURE assembly: relocatable deps dir. cargo resolves a config-relative `directory`
#     against the GRANDPARENT of the config file, so rebase the shipped config's @vendor@
#     onto a basename-anchored RELATIVE path -> parent($CARGO_HOME)/<basename>/vendor == $CARGO_HOME/vendor.
cargoDeps = runCommand ... ''
  cp -RL ${cargoVendor} $out/vendor; chmod -R u+w $out
  sed "s|@vendor@|${cargoHomeBasename}/vendor|g" $out/vendor/.cargo/config.toml > $out/config.toml
'';

# (3) OFFLINE build+test: env-only staging тАФ ONE `cp -RL <deps>` into $CARGO_HOME,
#     then CARGO_HOME + CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true + `cargo test --offline --frozen`. Needs `cc` on nativeBuildInputs.
```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A repo with `Cargo.toml`+`Cargo.lock` detects as ecosystem `rust`, package manager `cargo`
- [ ] `generateRustBuild` emits a `NixBuild` with `attrs { toolchain, deps, app }`, the `cargoHash` placed where the discovery loop reads it, the `@vendor@` тЖТ basename-anchored relative rebase, and `CARGO_NET_OFFLINE` on the offline step
- [ ] The store discovers `cargoHash` via the existing fake тЖТ mismatch тЖТ real probe (no new machinery) and lands it in `depsHash`
- [ ] Deps stage via the existing env-only `cp -RL` path (`storeSubpath: ""`); no `SandboxStaging` field added
- [ ] `cargo test` runs offline in the Sandbox and passes; staged deps are excluded from the worktree's git
- [ ] A pure Rust build derives egress `none`
- [ ] Registry stays exhaustive at `tsc`; the `impureInstall` iff `impuritySignal` invariant holds (cargo has neither)
- [ ] Tests: `generateRustBuild` (emitted `NixBuild` shape) and basic Rust detection, mirroring go/python prior art

## Blocked by
- dustcastle-gy5.1 тАФ Collapse provisioned deps hash

title: Collapse provisioned deps hash to a single depsHash
description:
## Parent
dustcastle-gy5 тАФ Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)

## What to build
Collapse the provisioned deps hash from three mutually-exclusive named fields into one honest field, as the prerequisite that lets Rust (and the other Ecosystems) stop smearing hash dispatch across the store and run layers.

Today `Provisioned` carries `vendorHash` / `npmDepsHash` / `pythonDepsHash` тАФ always exactly one set, immediately re-merged at read via `??` precedence тАФ with an `outputHashField` discriminator on each `PackageManagerDescriptor` routing the single discovered FOD hash into the right slot. Replace all of that with a single `depsHash: string` (`""` when impure / toolchain-only). Delete the `OutputHashField` type and the `outputHashField` descriptor field. Each Importer already maps `ctx.depsHash` onto its own Nix attr name internally, so the store/run layers never needed the attr name.

This contradicts the Registry's "owned in one place, not smeared across dispatch sites" principle; collapsing it makes adding an Ecosystem touch zero shared hash machinery.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Provisioned` exposes a single `depsHash: string`; the three per-ecosystem hash fields are gone
- [ ] `OutputHashField` type and the `outputHashField` field on `PackageManagerDescriptor` are deleted
- [ ] The store lands the discovered/supplied hash in `depsHash`; impure / toolchain-only provisions set `""`
- [ ] The run layer's GC project key reads `provisioned.depsHash || "toolchain"`
- [ ] Existing node / go / python provisioning and their tests stay green
- [ ] `tsc` passes (the closed-union exhaustiveness proof still holds)

## Blocked by
None - can start immediately

title: Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)
description:
## Problem Statement

dustcastle is Ecosystem-agnostic by design (CONTEXT.md), but its Ecosystem Registry today curates only node, go, and python. A user working in a Rust project gets no shared Toolchain, no pure vendored Project Deps in the Store, and no offline-capable Sandbox тАФ Rust simply isn't a language world dustcastle can provision. Rust developers can't back their agent sandboxes with the same deduplicated Nix Store every other Ecosystem enjoys.

## Solution

Add Rust as a first-class, internally-curated Ecosystem (ADR 0001 тАФ closed Registry, not a plugin system). A repo with a `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` is detected as Rust with a single `cargo` Package Manager; its Project Deps build pure-by-default into the Store as a fixed-output vendored derivation (ADR 0004); and its Sandbox stages those deps offline with no interface change. Rust is **Go-shaped on the build path** (single Package Manager, real lockfile, pure vendored FOD, no impurity signal) and **Node-shaped on the loose path** (a `Cargo.toml` without `Cargo.lock` is a loose manifest that pins-then-builds-pure via `cargo generate-lockfile`, ADR 0006c).

This work is gated by a completed throwaway spike (`spike-rust-cargo-fod/`, verdict GO) that proved the offline-staging mechanism end-to-end under the bundled nix-portable.

## User Stories

1. As a Rust developer, I want dustcastle to recognise my repo as Rust from its `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock`, so that I don't have to configure an Ecosystem (ADR 0001 тАФ the user never configures one).
2. As a Rust developer, I want my crates resolved from `Cargo.lock` and built into the shared Store once, so that every Sandbox draws them instantly without per-project rebuilds.
3. As a Rust developer with a committed `Cargo.lock`, I want a pure, hermetic, offline build, so that my deps are reproducible and the build reaches no network.
4. As a Rust developer, I want `cargo test` to run as the first green gate inside the Nix sandbox, so that a provisioned project is proven building-and-testing before an agent ever touches it (mirroring Go's `go test`).
5. As a Rust library author with **no** committed `Cargo.lock`, I want dustcastle to generate one for me (pin-then-pure) rather than failing, so that loose manifests are still provisioned тАФ strictly better than going impure (ADR 0006c).
6. As a Rust developer pinning a toolchain via `rust-toolchain.toml`, I want dustcastle to read that channel as my requested Toolchain version, so that the requested version is recorded into detection.
7. As a Rust developer using the legacy bare `rust-toolchain` file, I want that honoured as a fallback version source, so that older repos still resolve a version.
8. As a Rust developer who set `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml`, I want dustcastle to NOT mistake that MSRV floor for a Toolchain pin, so that my project isn't wrongly pinned to its minimum supported version.
9. As a Rust developer, I want my vendored deps staged into the Sandbox via the same env-only `cp -RL` mechanism every other Ecosystem uses, so that Rust adds no special staging machinery.
10. As a Rust developer, I want `cargo build`/`cargo test` in the Sandbox to run fully offline against the staged deps (`CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true`), so that the agent's environment needs no crates.io access for a pure build.
11. As a Rust developer with git dependencies, I want them vendored transparently under the single aggregate hash, so that I don't hand-maintain per-crate hashes.
12. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want adding Rust to be a deep, local change that the closed Registry derives from in one place, so that detection/store/impurity/pin/sandbox/egress stay exhaustive-by-construction (no runtime `default:` gaps).
13. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want the Rust Importer to reuse the existing fake-hash тЖТ mismatch тЖТ real-hash discovery loop verbatim, so that no new hash-discovery machinery is introduced.
14. As a reviewer, I want staged Rust deps excluded from the worktree's git, so that they never bloat my `git diff` or leak on merge (dustcastle-8dk).
15. As a security-conscious user, I want a pure Rust build to reach no network (`none`), so that an offline build can't silently talk to a registry.
16. As a security-conscious user, I want the loose-pin step's egress restricted to exactly the crates.io sparse index host it needs, so that lockfile generation can't reach anything else.
17. As a security-conscious user, I want git-dependency hosts added to the allowlist only when my manifest actually references them, so that the allowlist stays minimal and derived.
18. As an in-Sandbox coding agent, I want to reach my model provider on a pure Rust build (ADR 0010), so that I can work even when the build itself reaches nothing.
19. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want a single shared `CARGO_HOME` basename constant agreed between the Importer and the stager, so that the relocatable relative-path config resolves correctly without a `SandboxStaging` interface change.
20. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want the provisioned deps hash modelled as one honest `depsHash` field rather than three mutually-exclusive named fields, so that adding an Ecosystem stops smearing hash dispatch across the store and run layers.
21. As a Rust developer in a Cargo workspace, I want the directory holding `Cargo.lock` (the workspace root) treated as the buildable unit, so that a virtual-manifest workspace provisions from its single root lockfile.

## Implementation Decisions

### Step 1 тАФ Collapse the provisioned deps hash (prerequisite, folded into this PRD)

- Collapse the three mutually-exclusive hash fields on `Provisioned` (`vendorHash`/`npmDepsHash`/`pythonDepsHash`) into a single `depsHash: string` (`""` when impure / toolchain-only).
- Delete the `OutputHashField` type and the `outputHashField` field on `PackageManagerDescriptor`. The store's per-literal hash-landing dispatch collapses to assigning `depsHash`; the run layer's `??`-precedence read collapses to `provisioned.depsHash || "toolchain"`.
- Rationale: today one logical value (the FOD hash) is modelled as a discriminator + N parallel optional fields, always exactly-one-set and immediately re-merged at read тАФ contradicting the Registry's "owned in one place, not smeared across dispatch sites" principle. Each Importer already maps `ctx.depsHash` onto its own Nix attr name internally, so the store/run layers never needed the attr name. This makes Rust (and the other Ecosystems) touch zero shared hash machinery.

### Step 2 тАФ The Rust Importer (deep module: `generateRustBuild`)

- Pure function `spec тЖТ NixBuild`, mirroring `generateGoBuild`'s interface (`NixBuild` = expression + `attrs: { toolchain, deps, app }`).
- Build strategy: `rustPlatform.fetchCargoVendor` produces a fixed-output vendor derivation carrying **one aggregate `cargoHash`** тАФ the single network step, discoverable via the existing fake-hash тЖТ mismatch тЖТ real-hash probe. Chosen over `cargoLock`/`importCargoLock` (see ADR below): the aggregate hash is what the store's two-pass discovery loop pins, and git deps vendor transparently under it.
- The deps derivation is **self-contained and relocatable**: `$out/vendor` is the `fetchCargoVendor` tree (crates under `source-registry-0/`, plus its shipped `.cargo/config.toml` listing every source тАФ including git deps тАФ with an `@vendor@` placeholder); `$out/config.toml` is that shipped config with `@vendor@` rebased onto a **basename-anchored relative path**.
- Load-bearing finding from the spike (encoded precisely below): cargo resolves a config-relative `directory` against the **grandparent** of the config file, NOT the config's own dir. So a `$CARGO_HOME/config.toml` with naive `directory = "vendor"` resolves to `parent($CARGO_HOME)/vendor` and misses. Encoding the `CARGO_HOME` basename makes it land back inside `$CARGO_HOME/vendor`. This diverges deliberately from nixpkgs' `cargoSetupHook`, which substitutes an ABSOLUTE store path (not relocatable for env-only staging).
- The offline build/test derivation runs `cargo test` with `CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true` against the staged deps; `doCheck` (or an equivalent offline test step) is the first green gate. Requires `cc` on `nativeBuildInputs` (Rust links via cc even for pure-Rust crates).

Prototype-derived shape (the keepable parts of `spike-rust-cargo-fod/deps.nix` тАФ trimmed to the decisions; the Importer emits this, not the spike's throwaway harness):

```nix
# (1) FOD: network ON, ONE aggregate cargoHash (the store's two-pass discovery pins it).
cargoVendor = pkgs.rustPlatform.fetchCargoVendor { inherit src; name = ...; inherit hash; };

# Shared constant agreed with the stager (src/sandbox/plan.ts). NOT a SandboxStaging change.
cargoHomeBasename = "dustcastle-cargo-home";

# (2) PURE assembly: relocatable deps dir. @vendor@ rebased onto a basename-anchored
#     RELATIVE path so cargo's grandparent rule resolves each source back inside $CARGO_HOME.
cargoDeps = runCommand ... ''
  mkdir -p $out; cp -RL ${cargoVendor} $out/vendor; chmod -R u+w $out
  sed "s|@vendor@|${cargoHomeBasename}/vendor|g" $out/vendor/.cargo/config.toml > $out/config.toml
'';

# (3) OFFLINE build+test: env-only staging тАФ ONE `cp -RL <deps>` into $CARGO_HOME,
#     then CARGO_HOME + CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true + `cargo test --offline --frozen`.
```

### Step 3 тАФ The Rust descriptors (`RUST_ECOSYSTEM` + `cargo`)

- **Detection grain (`EcosystemDescriptor`)**: `ecosystem: "rust"`; `manifests: [Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock]`; `managers: ["cargo"]`; `defaultManager: "cargo"`; no `readDeclaredManager` (single Package Manager, like Go); `readToolchainVersion` = `readRustToolchainVersion`; **generic loose default** (no custom `isLooseManifest` тАФ a `Cargo.toml` with no lockfile IS the loose test, which the generic default covers); `sandbox` = env-only staging into the shared `CARGO_HOME` basename dir (`storeSubpath: ""`, like Go), env sets `CARGO_HOME` + `CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true` and puts the Toolchain `bin` on PATH ahead of the agent harness.
- **Dispatch grain (`PackageManagerDescriptor`)**: `packageManager: "cargo"`; `ecosystem: "rust"`; `lockfiles: [Cargo.lock, Cargo.toml]`; `generateBuild` тЖТ `generateRustBuild`; **no `impuritySignal`** (so no `impureInstall` тАФ pure, unconditional, like Go); **`lockOnlyResolve`** = `{ kind: "command", command: "cargo", args: ["generate-lockfile"], lockfile: "Cargo.lock" }` (the one place Rust diverges from Go); no `provisionGate`, no `exportFrontEnd`.
- Register in the Registry: add `"rust"` to the `Ecosystem` union and `"cargo"` to the `PackageManager` union; add `RUST_ECOSYSTEM` to `ECOSYSTEMS` and `RUST_MANAGERS` to the by-manager record (the `Record<PackageManager, тАж>` annotation makes the new member exhaustive-by-construction at tsc).

### Step 4 тАФ `readRustToolchainVersion` (small pure parser)

- Reads `rust-toolchain.toml`'s `[toolchain] channel` first, then the legacy bare `rust-toolchain` file (both via the version-file reader). Returns `undefined` when neither is present.
- Explicitly does NOT read `Cargo.toml`'s `rust-version` (that is an MSRV minimum, not a pin).
- The Importer ignores the version in v1 (builds against nixpkgs' default `rustc`/`cargo`), exactly as Node/Go ignore theirs today; the version is recorded into `Detection` for honesty and closure-keying. Honouring an arbitrary pinned version is a later Nix-side concern (rust-overlay) тАФ rustup is absent in the Sandbox, so a `rust-toolchain.toml` would be ignored there anyway.

### Step 5 тАФ Egress derivation

- Pure offline build тЖТ `none` (deps pre-vendored in the Store).
- Loose-pin (`cargo generate-lockfile`) Build Egress тЖТ **`index.crates.io` only** (sparse index resolution + checksums; no tarball download, so NO `static.crates.io`), plus any **git-dependency hosts** the manifest references.
- The vendor FOD (`fetchCargoVendor`, network-on inside Nix) reaches `index.crates.io` + `static.crates.io` (+ git hosts) тАФ this is the Nix-side fetch, distinct from in-Sandbox egress.
- NOT on the allowlist: the legacy git index `github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index` (sparse is the cargo тЙе1.70 default) and the `crates.io` API host (publish/search only). The loose-pin step's `CARGO_HOME` must be writable + online (it writes the sparse index cache; `--offline` fails on an empty cache).
- Agent Egress (ADR 0010) is unchanged тАФ the in-Sandbox agent reaches its model host regardless of build purity.

### ADR to write (under the ADR 0004 family)

"Cargo deps via `cargoHash`/`fetchCargoVendor`, not `cargoLock`/`importCargoLock`." Surprising (current Rust-on-Nix guidance reaches for `cargoLock` in-repo), hard to reverse (load-bearing on the store's two-pass discover-FOD-hash flow), real trade-off (traded `cargoLock`'s no-hash-churn ergonomics for the one discoverable aggregate hash the uniform provisioner needs + transparent git-dep vendoring). Record the grandparent-rule / basename-anchor finding as the rationale for diverging from `cargoSetupHook`'s absolute-path config.

## Testing Decisions

A good test asserts **external behaviour, not implementation details**: for the Importer, the shape of the emitted `NixBuild` (not the internal string-building); for the parser, the version it returns for given inputs; for detection, the `Detection` it concludes; for egress, the host set it derives. All four chosen for tests:

1. **`generateRustBuild`** (new `src/nix/rust.test.ts`) тАФ assert the emitted `NixBuild`: `attrs` are `{ toolchain, deps, app }`; the `cargoHash` is placed where the discovery loop reads it; the deps assembly performs the `@vendor@` тЖТ basename-anchored relative rebase; the offline step carries `CARGO_NET_OFFLINE`. Prior art: `src/nix/go.test.ts`, `src/nix/python.test.ts`.
2. **`readRustToolchainVersion`** (new `src/ecosystems/rust-version.test.ts`) тАФ `rust-toolchain.toml` channel read; legacy `rust-toolchain` fallback; `Cargo.toml` `rust-version` ignored; `undefined` when absent. Prior art: `src/ecosystems/python-version.test.ts`.
3. **Rust detection** (extend `src/detect/detect.test.ts` + `src/ecosystems/ecosystems.test.ts`) тАФ `Cargo.lock` тЖТ `cargo`; loose `Cargo.toml`-without-lock flagged `loose`; workspace-root-with-`Cargo.lock` as the buildable unit; Registry exhaustiveness (every `PackageManager` incl. `cargo` has a descriptor; the `impureInstall` iff `impuritySignal` invariant still holds with cargo having neither). Prior art: `src/detect/detect.test.ts`, `src/ecosystems/ecosystems.test.ts`.
4. **cargo egress** (extend `src/sandbox/egress.test.ts`) тАФ pin derives `index.crates.io` only; FOD derives `index.crates.io` + `static.crates.io`; git-dep manifest adds its git host; pure build derives `none`. Prior art: `src/sandbox/egress.test.ts`.

## Out of Scope

- Honouring a pinned Rust toolchain version in the Importer (rust-overlay / fenix). v1 reads the version into detection but builds against nixpkgs' default `rustc`/`cargo`, exactly like Node/Go today.
- Impure Rust builds. Rust has no `impuritySignal` in v1 (no install-script path); `build.rs` / `*-sys` system-library needs are a Toolchain-curation concern, not a per-build impurity signal.
- Deep Cargo workspace member resolution (per-member provisioning). The directory holding `Cargo.lock` (the workspace root) is the buildable unit; member subdirs are not independently provisioned тАФ paralleling how Go ignores `go.work` in v1.
- Alternative Rust package managers. `cargo` is the sole Package Manager.
- The microVM Boundary and any non-container isolation (ADR 0003 тАФ container boundary for v1).

## Further Notes

- Gating spike: `spike-rust-cargo-fod/` (NOTES.md verdict GO, deps.nix = the keepable Importer shape, run.sh/fixture/spike.log throwaway). Delete it once Step 2 and the ADR fold in the two learnings (grandparent-rule basename anchor; rebase `fetchCargoVendor`'s shipped config rather than hand-writing one). All four gates passed under bundled nix-portable (bwrap), nixpkgs cargo/rustc 1.95.0.
- Caveats flagged but not exercised by the spike, to verify before shipping: prove a **git-dependency fixture** vendors under the same aggregate hash and is covered free by the `@vendor@` rebase; confirm `cc` on `nativeBuildInputs` for the offline link.
- CONTEXT.md needs no new term тАФ `rust` is an instance of the existing *Ecosystem* vocabulary and `cargo` of *Package Manager* (the glossary already lists Rust as an example).
- Relevant ADRs: 0001 (closed Registry / internal curation), 0004 (pure-default, explicit impurity), 0006 (lockfile-router detection) incl. 0006b (toolchain version) and 0006c (loose manifest pin-then-pure), 0005/0010/0011 (egress).

title: ADR: cargo deps via cargoHash/fetchCargoVendor, not cargoLock/importCargoLock
description:
## Parent
dustcastle-gy5 тАФ Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)

## What to build
Record the architectural decision behind the Rust Importer as an ADR under the 0004 family: dustcastle vendors cargo deps via `cargoHash` / `fetchCargoVendor`, not `cargoLock` / `importCargoLock`.

Capture the trade-off: `cargoLock`/`importCargoLock` is the trendy in-repo choice (no aggregate-hash churn), but it has no single discoverable FOD hash тАФ the exact thing the store's two-pass discover loop pins тАФ and forces manual `outputHashes` for git deps. `cargoHash`/`fetchCargoVendor` gives one discoverable `got: sha256-тАж` hash and vendors git deps transparently. Also record the spike's grandparent-rule / basename-anchor finding as the rationale for diverging from nixpkgs' `cargoSetupHook` (which substitutes an absolute, non-relocatable store path) тАФ dustcastle rebases onto a relative basename-anchored path so the deps dir rides env-only staging.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A new ADR in the 0004 family states the decision, alternatives (`cargoLock`/`importCargoLock`), and consequences
- [ ] It records why the aggregate discoverable hash is load-bearing for the store's two-pass flow
- [ ] It records the grandparent-rule / basename-anchor divergence from `cargoSetupHook`
- [ ] CONTEXT.md is unchanged (no new domain term тАФ `rust`/`cargo` are instances of existing vocabulary)

## Blocked by
- dustcastle-gy5.2 тАФ Rust pure happy-path

title: Rust git-dependency vendoring + derived git-host egress
description:
## Parent
dustcastle-gy5 тАФ Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)

## What to build
Confirm and cover Rust git dependencies end-to-end: they vendor transparently under the single aggregate hash, and their git host is added to Build Egress only when the manifest references one.

`fetchCargoVendor` vendors git deps under the same aggregate `cargoHash` and lists them in the shipped `.cargo/config.toml`, so the `@vendor@` rebase (slice 2) covers them for free тАФ prove this with a git-dependency fixture (the spike used pure crates.io only). For egress, when a manifest references a git dependency, the vendor FOD's Build Egress includes that git host (e.g. `github.com:443`) alongside `index.crates.io` + `static.crates.io`; a manifest with no git deps adds no git host. The allowlist stays derived and minimal.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A fixture with a git dependency provisions: the git crate is vendored under the aggregate hash and resolved offline via the rebased config
- [ ] The vendor FOD Build Egress adds the referenced git host only when a git dep is present
- [ ] A manifest with no git deps adds no git host to the allowlist
- [ ] Legacy git index (`github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index`) and the `crates.io` API host are NOT added (sparse default)
- [ ] Tests: egress git-host derivation + the git-dep fixture

## Blocked by
- dustcastle-gy5.2 тАФ Rust pure happy-path

title: Loose Cargo.toml pin-then-pure via cargo generate-lockfile
description:
## Parent
dustcastle-gy5 тАФ Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)

## What to build
The loose-manifest pin-then-pure path for Rust: a `Cargo.toml` with no `Cargo.lock` is provisioned by first generating a lockfile, then building pure тАФ strictly better than going impure (ADR 0006c).

Detection flags the directory `loose` via the generic loose default (manifest present, no lockfile). The `cargo` descriptor carries `lockOnlyResolve = { kind: "command", command: "cargo", args: ["generate-lockfile"], lockfile: "Cargo.lock" }`. The pin step runs online under a minimal Build Egress allowlist of `index.crates.io` only тАФ `generate-lockfile` resolves and reads checksums from the sparse index but downloads no tarballs, so `static.crates.io` is NOT needed. Its `CARGO_HOME` must be writable + online (it writes the sparse index cache; `--offline` fails on an empty cache). After pinning, the normal pure vendored build (slice 2) takes over.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A `Cargo.toml` without `Cargo.lock` is detected as `loose`
- [ ] The `cargo` descriptor exposes `lockOnlyResolve` running `cargo generate-lockfile` producing `Cargo.lock`
- [ ] The loose-pin Build Egress derives `index.crates.io` only (no `static.crates.io`)
- [ ] After pinning, the project builds pure via the slice-2 path
- [ ] Tests: loose Rust detection + the pin-step egress host set

## Blocked by
- dustcastle-gy5.2 тАФ Rust pure happy-path

title: Rust Toolchain version reading (rust-toolchain.toml / legacy; ignore rust-version MSRV)
description:
## Parent
dustcastle-gy5 тАФ Add Rust Ecosystem to the Registry (cargo)

## What to build
Read the requested Rust Toolchain version into detection, from the idiomatic version files тАФ without mistaking the MSRV floor for a pin.

`readRustToolchainVersion` reads `rust-toolchain.toml`'s `[toolchain] channel` first, then the legacy bare `rust-toolchain` file (both via the version-file reader), and returns `undefined` when neither is present. It explicitly does NOT read `Cargo.toml`'s `rust-version` тАФ that is an MSRV minimum, not a pin. The resolved version is recorded into `Detection` for honesty and closure-keying; the Importer still builds against nixpkgs' default `rustc`/`cargo` in v1 (exactly as Node/Go ignore their version today).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A repo with `rust-toolchain.toml` populates `Detection.toolchainVersion` from `[toolchain] channel`
- [ ] A repo with only the legacy `rust-toolchain` file falls back to that channel string
- [ ] `Cargo.toml` `rust-version` is NOT read as the Toolchain version
- [ ] No version file тЖТ `Detection.toolchainVersion` is undefined
- [ ] The Importer's output is unchanged by the version (v1 builds against nixpkgs default)
- [ ] Tests: `rust-version` parser cases, mirroring `python-version` prior art

## Blocked by
- dustcastle-gy5.2 тАФ Rust pure happy-path

title: Exclude dustcastle's staging dirs from the per-issue worktree's git
description:
## Problem

dustcastle stages Project Deps into the worktree on sandbox-ready (`setupFor` тЖТ
`stageCommands`): `node_modules/` (node), `vendor/` (go), `site/` (python). These
are pure build artifacts тАФ re-staged fresh from the read-only Store on every run
(self-healing `rm -rf <dir>; cp -RL тАж; chmod`).

The sandboxтЖТhost channel is git only: each issue runs in a clean per-issue worktree
on `sandcastle/issue-<id>`, the agent/reviewer accumulate commits, and the merge
phase merges those branches. Only COMMITTED content crosses the boundary.

The staging dir `site/` is a dustcastle-invented name, NOT a Python convention, so a
normal Python repo's `.gitignore` does not list it. Consequences when the implementer
runs `git add -A && git commit` (a routine agent move):

1. **Reviewer diff bloat (primary harm).** The reviewer prompt runs
   `git diff {{BASE_BRANCH}}...` (see `reviewArgs`, orchestrate.ts). A committed
   `site/` floods that diff with thousands of vendored dep files тАФ the real change
   is buried, review quality drops, reviewer context is burned.
2. **Leak into main.** The merge then carries the staged site-packages tree into the
   base branch.

`node_modules/` is safe (every JS repo ignores it); `vendor/` is often intentionally
committed in Go (conventional). `site/` is the real exposure.

## Fix

Before handing the worktree to the agent (or as the first step of the deps-staging
hook, ahead of the `cp`), append the active ecosystem's staging dir(s) to the
worktree's `.git/info/exclude`. This is local and untracked тАФ it does NOT modify the
project's committed `.gitignore`, and it cannot be committed by the agent. The
staging dir is already declared on the Ecosystem descriptor's `sandbox` facet
(`stageDir`), so the exclude list derives from the Registry like everything else.

## Out of scope

- Changing where deps stage (must stay in-tree for tooling: PYTHONPATH=site, vendor/, node_modules/).
- The committed project `.gitignore` (untouched тАФ this is `.git/info/exclude`).
design:
## Recommended approach: a worktree-local git EXCLUDE rule, derived from the Registry

Add a git exclude entry for the active ecosystem's `stageDir` тАФ NOT an edit to the
project's tracked `.gitignore`. The entry is read from `ecosystemFor(detection.ecosystem).sandbox.stageDir`
(the same facet the staging reads), so it stays in sync as DATA, never a hardcoded "site".

### Where to inject
As the FIRST step of the deps-staging hook, ahead of the `cp` тАФ the same onSandboxReady
list dustcastle already prepends (src/run/index.ts:475). This guarantees the exclude
exists whenever staging happens and ties it to the exact dir being staged. Sketch:

    # first staging step, before the existing chmod/rm/cp/chmod:
    f="$(git --no-optional-locks rev-parse --git-path info/exclude)"
    grep -qxF "<stageDir>" "$f" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s\n' "<stageDir>" >> "$f"

### Two correctness facts that make this safe AND sufficient

1. Ignore rules NEVER affect already-tracked files. A Go project that intentionally
   commits `vendor/` is unaffected (git keeps tracking it; the exclude is a no-op there);
   a project that does NOT track it is protected. So node_modules/site/vendor can be
   excluded UNIFORMLY with no per-ecosystem opt-out and no risk to a vendored repo.

2. The exclude MUST be worktree-scoped, not host-scoped (the one thing to verify before
   implementing). `info/exclude` lives in git's COMMON dir, which linked worktrees share тАФ
   so a naive write could pollute the host repo or sibling per-issue worktrees. Resolve by:
   - if sandcastle's worktrees are isolated checkouts (own .git) тЖТ info/exclude is already
     private; done.
   - if they are real linked worktrees sharing the common dir тЖТ use a per-worktree exclude:
     `extensions.worktreeConfig=true` + `git config --worktree core.excludesFile <path in the
     worktree's private gitdir>`, written before the agent runs.
   Confirm sandcastle's worktree model first (orchestrate.ts says it hands us a "clean checkout").

### Rejected alternatives
- Stage Python out-of-tree (`/tmp/site` + absolute PYTHONPATH). It WOULD work for Python and
  would dodge git entirely, but it introduces a new LOCATION-KIND axis (worktree-relative vs
  absolute-scratch) that only Python could use: node's module resolution walks the dir tree for
  `node_modules/` (NODE_PATH is legacy/unreliable) and go's `-mod=vendor` requires `vendor/` at
  the module root тАФ both are pinned worktree-relative by their toolchains. So /tmp helps one
  ecosystem while forcing a per-ecosystem branch in the facet + stageCommands тАФ the exact
  special-casing the bbg epic removed. The dir NAME being per-ecosystem is fine (already data);
  the LOCATION KIND staying uniform (relative, in-tree) is what we preserve.
- Edit the project's tracked `.gitignore` (pollutes the repo; could itself be committed).
- Rely only on a "don't commit staged deps" line in the implement prompt тАФ soft guard, not a
  guarantee, and the reviewer `git diff` still shows them if committed. Acceptable only as
  defense-in-depth layered on top of the exclude, not as the mechanism.

### Verification
- Unit: the staging hook for each ecosystem writes its `stageDir` into the exclude (derived
  from the facet, not a literal).
- Regression: a tracked `vendor/` stays tracked (asserts fact #1).
- E2e (gated): after an implement run with staged deps, the worktree `git status`/`git diff`
  shows no staged-dep noise тАФ the reviewer diff stays scoped to the real change.
acceptance_criteria: site/ (and node_modules/, vendor/ when staged) are added to the per-issue worktree's .git/info/exclude before the agent runs, so an agent 'git add -A' cannot capture staged deps; reviewer 'git diff BASE...' stays scoped to the real change; nothing reaches main via merge; the project's committed .gitignore is NOT modified; verified by a test asserting the exclude file carries the active ecosystem's stageDir.
notes:
Primary harm is reviewer diff-bloat (the review prompt runs git diff BASE...), not just the leak-into-main on merge. site/ is the live exposure because it is a dustcastle-invented name a normal Python repo does not gitignore (unlike node_modules, and unlike vendor which Go often commits intentionally).
Verified in sandcastle: its untracked-sync runs 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' (chunk-5VM5QZ26.js) and commits via 'git format-patch base..HEAD' тАФ both honor .git/info/exclude, and the agent's own 'git add' honors it too. So this fix composes with sandcastle (no sandcastle change needed); sandcastle just never populated the exclude with dustcastle's stageDirs.
EMPIRICAL (host simulation in a real linked worktree created via 'git worktree add', using the exact command setupFor emits): WORKS тАФ before, 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' (sandcastle's untracked-sync command) listed site/pkg/__init__.py; after running the emitted command it returns empty and 'git status --short' is empty, so staged deps are invisible to both the agent's git and sandcastle's sync. Idempotent: running it 3x leaves exactly one 'site' line. Safety confirmed: a TRACKED vendor/keep.txt still shows as modified after excluding 'vendor' (ignore rules never affect tracked files), so a Go repo that commits vendor/ is unaffected. CAVEAT confirmed (not hypothetical): for a linked worktree 'git rev-parse --git-path info/exclude' resolves to the SHARED common git dir (the host repo's .git/info/exclude), so the entries are repo-global and land in the host's local (never-committed, never-pushed) exclude тАФ benign since every issue wants the same exclusion, but the per-worktree core.excludesFile variant would isolate it if ever needed. RESIDUAL UNKNOWN the host sim can't settle: whether the container mounts the host common .git WRITABLE (the append needs write access). The agent commits in-container so git must be writable there, but only a real run / the DUSTCASTLE_E2E-gated path proves the mount topology.
close_reason: Implemented (commit 18641db) via TDD: setupFor prepends an idempotent .git/info/exclude write for the active Ecosystem's stageDir (node_modules/site/vendor), on both pure and impure paths, derived from the sandbox facet. Composes with sandcastle's --exclude-standard untracked-sync. 479/479 unit tests pass, typecheck clean. NOTE: chose the in-container info/exclude append (mechanism A); double-run idempotency asserted at the unit level via the grep-guard structure, true execution verification left to the DUSTCASTLE_E2E-gated path.

title: impureInstall on PackageManagerDescriptor (+ fix python impure staging)
description:
## Parent

dustcastle-bbg тАФ Migrate sandbox staging onto the Ecosystem Registry

## What to build

Move the **impure in-container install** onto the dispatch grain (`PackageManagerDescriptor`), make `setupFor`'s impure path ecosystem-agnostic, and in doing so **close a latent bug**: python builds can go impure but `setupFor` only handles node.

### Why this is a bugfix, not just a relocation

Impurity is reachable for any Package Manager whose descriptor carries an `impuritySignal`, and `decideImpurity` is ecosystem-agnostic. That set is node (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun) **and python (pip/uv/poetry)** тАФ only go has no `impuritySignal`. But today `setupFor`'s impure branch lives inside `if (ecosystem === "node")`; a python impure build (e.g. a uv/poetry project with an sdist dependency) falls through to the *pure* python branch and runs `cp` from an empty `depsStorePath` тАФ broken staging. Likely never hit yet because uv/poetry are recent, but it is a live gap.

### The change

- Add an optional `impureInstall?: readonly string[]` field to `PackageManagerDescriptor` тАФ the frozen/immutable in-container install command(s) for that manager, run under scoped egress when the deps were not pre-assembled in the Store. It is **optional**: present for every manager that carries an `impuritySignal` (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun/pip/uv/poetry), absent for go (which has no `impuritySignal` and can never go impure).
- **node managers**: single-command lists тАФ `["npm ci"]`, `["pnpm install --frozen-lockfile"]`, `["yarn install --frozen-lockfile"]`, `["bun install --frozen-lockfile"]` (bun's stays for uniformity though its `provisionGate` fires first, exactly like its never-realized `generateBuild`).
- **python managers**: installs land in `./site` (the same directory the pure path stages into and `PYTHONPATH` points at тАФ the run env is identical pure or impure). The lists are **constructed in `python.ts`**, not hand-written, by reusing each manager's existing `exportFrontEnd` plus one shared pip-into-site command, so the export command is single-sourced (no duplication of `exportFrontEnd` data):
  - a shared constant: `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt --target site`
  - `pip` тЖТ `[<shared pip install>]`
  - `uv` тЖТ `[<uv export, derived from uv's exportFrontEnd>, <shared pip install>]`
  - `poetry` тЖТ `[<poetry export, derived from poetry's exportFrontEnd>, <shared pip install>]`
  - The install must not resolve versions beyond the committed/exported requirements (the no-drift invariant); the hash-pinned export makes `--require-hashes` the natural enforcement.
- **`setupFor`** impure path becomes ecosystem-agnostic and uniform: it reads `packageManagerDescriptor(detection.packageManager).impureInstall` and returns it, regardless of Ecosystem. Remove the node-only `if` gate around the impure branch, and remove the `IMPURE_INSTALL` record together with its silent `?? "npm ci"` fallback. If a manager somehow reaches the impure path with no `impureInstall` (should be impossible given the invariant below), throw a clear error rather than mis-staging.

### Exhaustiveness (replaces the wrong "fails at tsc" claim)

Because `impureInstall` is legitimately optional (go has none), the guarantee is expressed as a **descriptor invariant test** rather than the type system: *a Package Manager carries `impureInstall` if and only if it carries `impuritySignal`.* That keeps a half-added manager honest тАФ any manager that can reach the impure path is proven to have an install command.

This slice is independent of the staging/run-env slices (different descriptor, different code path), though it edits the same `setupFor` function as the staging slice тАФ whoever lands second rebases the function.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `PackageManagerDescriptor` has an optional `impureInstall?: readonly string[]`, present for npm/pnpm/yarn/bun/pip/uv/poetry and absent for go.
- [ ] Python managers' `impureInstall` lists are constructed from their existing `exportFrontEnd` (where present) plus one shared `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt --target site` constant тАФ the export command is not duplicated as a literal string.
- [ ] `setupFor`'s impure path is ecosystem-agnostic: it reads `impureInstall` from the Package Manager descriptor for node AND python; the node-only `if` gate is gone.
- [ ] A python impure build stages correctly into `./site` (the latent bug is closed) тАФ verified end-to-end, not just unit.
- [ ] The `IMPURE_INSTALL` record and its `?? "npm ci"` fallback are removed; reaching the impure path with no `impureInstall` throws a clear error.
- [ ] `ecosystems.test.ts` asserts the invariant: a manager has `impureInstall` iff it has `impuritySignal` (go has neither; the other seven have both), and that each command is correct.
- [ ] `plan.test.ts` asserts `planSandbox` emits the same node impure install commands as before, AND the new python impure install (export + pip-into-site) per python manager.
- [ ] Full test suite green.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately (parallel to the staging and run-env slices; edits the same `setupFor` function as the staging slice, so the second to land rebases).
close_reason: Implemented via TDD workflow: sandbox staging now derives from the Ecosystem Registry (sandbox facet + stageCommands, run-env facet, impureInstall on PackageManagerDescriptor + python impure bugfix). 474/474 unit tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Run-env facet on EcosystemDescriptor (+ CONTEXT.md derive-list)
description:
## Parent

dustcastle-bbg тАФ Migrate sandbox staging onto the Ecosystem Registry

## What to build

Move the per-Ecosystem **run environment** onto the same `EcosystemDescriptor.sandbox` facet introduced by the staging slice, retiring the `envFor` if-ladder.

Add an `env` member to the `sandbox` facet that produces the run-environment map for an Ecosystem given the Toolchain `bin` path тАФ the Toolchain on PATH plus the writable cache vars currently hardcoded per Ecosystem in `envFor` (e.g. node's `NPM_CONFIG_CACHE`/`XDG_CACHE_HOME`, python's `PYTHONPATH`/`PIP_CACHE_DIR`, go's `GOFLAGS`/`GOPROXY`/`GOCACHE`/etc.). Declare it on node, python, and go. Rewrite `envFor` to resolve the Ecosystem descriptor's `env` and delete the `if (ecosystem === тАж)` ladder.

With `setupFor` (staging slice) and `envFor` (this slice) both reading descriptors, the Sandbox site now derives from the Registry. **Also fold in the docs catch-up**: update the Ecosystem Registry entry in CONTEXT.md so its derive-list reads `detect / store / impurity / pin / nix / sandbox`.

Behaviour must be identical: the exact env keys and values emitted per Ecosystem stay the same.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The `sandbox` facet has an `env` member returning the run-environment map per Ecosystem, declared for node, python, and go.
- [ ] `envFor` reads the descriptor's `env`; the `if (ecosystem === тАж)` ladder in `envFor` is gone.
- [ ] CONTEXT.md's Ecosystem Registry glossary entry lists `sandbox` among the deriving sites.
- [ ] `ecosystems.test.ts` asserts each Ecosystem descriptor's `env` carries the correct PATH and cache vars.
- [ ] `plan.test.ts` still asserts `planSandbox` emits the same env per Ecosystem (behaviour-preserving guard).
- [ ] Full test suite green; emitted env byte-identical to before.

## Blocked by

dustcastle-bbg.1 (Pure-staging facet + deep stageCommands) тАФ the `sandbox` facet must exist before `env` is added to it.
close_reason: Implemented via TDD workflow: sandbox staging now derives from the Ecosystem Registry (sandbox facet + stageCommands, run-env facet, impureInstall on PackageManagerDescriptor + python impure bugfix). 474/474 unit tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Pure-staging facet + deep stageCommands on the Ecosystem Registry
description:
## Parent

dustcastle-bbg тАФ Migrate sandbox staging onto the Ecosystem Registry

## What to build

Make the **pure** Project-Deps staging declared by the Ecosystem Registry instead of a hand-rolled `if (ecosystem === тАж)` ladder in `setupFor`.

Add a `sandbox` facet to `EcosystemDescriptor` carrying the two things that vary per Ecosystem on the pure path:

- `stageDir` тАФ the worktree directory deps are staged into (`node_modules` for node, `site` for python, `vendor` for go).
- `storeSubpath` тАФ the path within `depsStorePath` to copy from (`node_modules` for node, `site` for python, none/empty for go which copies the whole deps store path).

Declare it on the node, python, and go descriptors. Then extract a single pure function тАФ `stageCommands(sandboxFacet, depsStorePath)` тАФ that emits the self-healing staging command list **once**: chmod-writable-then-rm the target (so an interrupted stage can't leave an unremovable read-only directory), `cp -тАжL` from the resolved source (`depsStorePath` joined with `storeSubpath`, or `depsStorePath` itself when there is no subpath), then chmod the result writable. Rewrite `setupFor`'s pure branch to call it and delete the per-Ecosystem if-ladder for the pure path.

End-to-end behaviour must be identical: the exact command strings and `cp` flags emitted per Ecosystem stay the same. This is a relocation of where the knowledge is declared, not a change to what runs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `EcosystemDescriptor` has a `sandbox` facet with `stageDir` and `storeSubpath`, declared for node, python, and go.
- [ ] A pure `stageCommands(facet, depsStorePath)` function emits the self-healing clear (chmod before rm), the `cp -тАжL` from the resolved source, and the trailing chmod тАФ written once, not per Ecosystem.
- [ ] Go's "copy the whole deps store path" case is expressed as descriptor data (no subpath), not an `else` branch.
- [ ] `setupFor`'s pure path reads the descriptor via `stageCommands`; the pure-path `if (ecosystem === тАж)` ladder is gone.
- [ ] `stageCommands` has unit tests covering the self-healing clear, the node/python subpath join, and Go's whole-directory copy.
- [ ] `ecosystems.test.ts` asserts each Ecosystem descriptor carries the correct `sandbox` staging fields.
- [ ] `plan.test.ts` still asserts `planSandbox` emits the same pure-path setup commands per Ecosystem (behaviour-preserving guard).
- [ ] Full test suite green; emitted commands byte-identical to before.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
notes:
Clarification (cp-flag uniformity): today node/python emit `cp -RL` and go emits `cp -rL`. In coreutils `-r` and `-R` are identical for `cp`, so the single `stageCommands` may emit one flag (`-RL`) for all three. This makes go's emitted command differ by one character from today тАФ that is a harmless no-op change. Resolution: standardize on `cp -RL` in `stageCommands` and relax the byte-identical assertion to allow go's `-rL`тЖТ`-RL` flag normalization (behaviour is unchanged). Do NOT carry the cp flag in the facet just to preserve the byte.
Coordination with .3: this slice rewrites ONLY the pure staging tail. Leave the node-only impure branch (`if (impure) return [...]`) exactly as-is тАФ generalizing the impure path is .3's job. Both slices edit setupFor, so the second to land rebases the function.
close_reason: Implemented via TDD workflow: sandbox staging now derives from the Ecosystem Registry (sandbox facet + stageCommands, run-env facet, impureInstall on PackageManagerDescriptor + python impure bugfix). 474/474 unit tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Migrate sandbox staging onto the Ecosystem Registry
description:
## Problem Statement

When dustcastle stands up a Sandbox, `src/sandbox/plan.ts` decides тАФ per Ecosystem тАФ how to stage Project Deps out of the read-only Store and what run environment to put on PATH. Today that knowledge lives in **three hand-rolled `if (ecosystem === тАж)` ladders** in `plan.ts`: `setupFor` (the staging commands), `envFor` (the run env), and `IMPURE_INSTALL` (the in-container install command, keyed by a free `string` with a `?? "npm ci"` fallback).

This is the one dispatch site that never migrated onto the **Ecosystem Registry**. CONTEXT.md defines the Registry as the set of descriptors that the `detect / store / impurity / pin / nix` sites all *derive* from тАФ and `sandbox` is conspicuously absent from that list. The consequences are felt today:

- The self-healing staging sequence (`chmod -R u+w тАж 2>/dev/null; rm -rf тАж; cp -RL тАж; chmod -R u+w тАж`) is **copy-pasted three times**. The subtle read-only-poisoning fix тАФ chmod-before-rm so an interrupted stage can't leave an unremovable read-only `node_modules` тАФ must be made, and kept in sync, in every branch.
- Adding an Ecosystem (e.g. Rust) means editing `plan.ts` in two places that have **no compile-time tie to the Registry**, the exact "smeared across dispatch sites" failure the glossary warns against.
- `IMPURE_INSTALL`'s `?? "npm ci"` fallback is precisely the runtime-default the rest of the Registry deliberately abolished: every other dispatch site is exhaustive by construction (a half-added Package Manager fails at `tsc`). A new manager here would silently fall back to `npm ci` instead of failing the build.

## Solution

Finish the migration the codebase already committed to for five other sites: make the per-Sandbox knowledge **declared by the Registry descriptors**, and reduce `plan.ts` to a uniform reader.

The switches split cleanly along the two descriptor grains the Registry already defines:

- **Detection grain (`EcosystemDescriptor`)** owns the pure staging target and the run env. `node_modules` / `site` / `vendor` is *manager-agnostic* (every JS Importer publishes the same `node_modules` layout тАФ the existing code comment says so), so it belongs on the Ecosystem, not the Package Manager.
- **Dispatch grain (`PackageManagerDescriptor`)** owns the impure install command тАФ the same grain that already owns `generateBuild` and `impuritySignal`. `npm ci` vs `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` is per-Package-Manager.

After the change, `setupFor`/`envFor` read their descriptor and a single deep `stageCommands` function emits the self-healing staging sequence once. The glossary's derive-list gains `sandbox`, so the map matches the territory.

## User Stories

1. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want all per-Ecosystem Sandbox staging knowledge declared on the Ecosystem descriptor, so that I can answer "what does node do at sandbox-ready?" by reading `node.ts` alone instead of bouncing to `plan.ts`.
2. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want the self-healing staging sequence written once, so that a fix to the read-only-poisoning behaviour lands in one place rather than three copy-pasted branches.
3. As a dustcastle maintainer adding a new Ecosystem (e.g. Rust), I want to declare its stage directory, store subpath, and run env on its descriptor, so that the Sandbox stages it correctly without my editing `plan.ts`.
4. As a dustcastle maintainer adding a new Package Manager, I want its impure-install command to be a required descriptor field, so that omitting it fails at `tsc` instead of silently falling back to `npm ci`.
5. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want `stageCommands` to be a pure function over a small facet, so that I can unit-test the staging algorithm (self-healing clear, store-subpath join, Go's whole-directory copy) without constructing a full Detection + Provisioned.
6. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want each Ecosystem and Package Manager descriptor's sandbox fields asserted in `ecosystems.test.ts`, so that a half-added Ecosystem is caught by a failing test.
7. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want `plan.test.ts` to keep asserting that `planSandbox` emits the same setup commands and env per Ecosystem after the rewrite, so that the refactor is provably behaviour-preserving.
8. As a coding agent running inside the Sandbox, I want my Project Deps staged identically to before (node_modules / site / vendor materialised from the Store with the right modes), so that the refactor changes nothing I can observe.
9. As a coding agent on an impure `allow` JS build, I want the correct frozen-lockfile install command run for the Package Manager that signalled, so that lifecycle scripts run under scoped egress exactly as before.
10. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want the run environment (Toolchain on PATH plus writable caches off the read-only Store) declared per Ecosystem on its descriptor, so that PATH/cache decisions live with the rest of that Ecosystem's knowledge.
11. As a future architecture reviewer (human or AI), I want CONTEXT.md's Ecosystem Registry entry to list `sandbox` among the deriving sites, so that the glossary is the accurate map of which sites key on descriptors.
12. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want the `IMPURE_INSTALL` record and its `?? "npm ci"` fallback removed, so that the impure-install dispatch enjoys the same exhaustive-by-construction guarantee as `generateBuild` and `impuritySignal`.
13. As a dustcastle maintainer, I want Go's "copy the whole deps store path" case (no subpath) expressed as descriptor data, so that it stops being an `else` branch and becomes a uniform descriptor entry.

## Implementation Decisions

- **New facet on the detection grain.** `EcosystemDescriptor` gains a `sandbox` facet carrying exactly what varies per Ecosystem:
  - `stageDir` тАФ the worktree directory deps are staged into (`node_modules` | `site` | `vendor`).
  - `storeSubpath` тАФ the path within `depsStorePath` to copy from (`node_modules`, `site`, or empty/none for Go which copies the whole deps store path).
  - `env` тАФ produces the run-environment map (Toolchain `bin` on PATH plus the writable cache vars currently in `envFor`).
- **One deep staging module.** A pure function тАФ `stageCommands(sandboxFacet, depsStorePath)` тАФ emits the self-healing staging command list once: chmod-writable-then-rm the target (so an interrupted stage can't leave an unremovable read-only dir), `cp -тАжL` from the resolved source, then chmod the result writable. The source path is `depsStorePath` joined with `storeSubpath`, or `depsStorePath` itself when there is no subpath (Go).
- **Impure install moves to the dispatch grain.** `PackageManagerDescriptor` gains an `impureInstall` field (the frozen/immutable in-container install command for that manager). `IMPURE_INSTALL` and its `?? "npm ci"` fallback are removed. The field's presence/typing makes the dispatch exhaustive by construction, matching `generateBuild` / `impuritySignal`.
- **`plan.ts` becomes a uniform reader.** `setupFor` resolves the Ecosystem descriptor for the pure path (calling `stageCommands`) and the Package Manager descriptor for the impure path; `envFor` resolves the Ecosystem descriptor's `env`. Both `if (ecosystem === тАж)` ladders are deleted.
- **Grain rationale is load-bearing and recorded in prose:** staging target + run env are Ecosystem-grain because they are manager-agnostic; the impure install is Package-Manager-grain because it is the manager's own command. This mirrors the existing split between `EcosystemDescriptor` (detection) and `PackageManagerDescriptor` (dispatch).
- **No ADR conflict.** ADR 0006 owns the detectionтЖТmanager router and is untouched. ADR 0001 frames extending descriptors as dustcastle's deep, local change, which is exactly this. ADR 0004/0005/0010 (purity, egress, agent-egress) are behaviourally unchanged тАФ the emitted commands and env are identical.
- **Behaviour-preserving.** The exact command strings, `cp` flags, and env keys/values emitted per Ecosystem and Package Manager must be unchanged; this is a pure relocation of where the knowledge is declared.

## Testing Decisions

A good test here asserts **external behaviour** тАФ the commands and environment dustcastle hands sandcastle тАФ not the internal shape of the descriptor objects. Tests should read like "for a node pure build, `planSandbox` emits these staging commands and this env," not "the descriptor has a `sandbox` key."

- **M2 `stageCommands` (deep, primary surface):** unit-test the pure function directly тАФ that it emits the self-healing clear (chmod before rm), joins `storeSubpath` onto `depsStorePath` correctly for node/python, and copies the whole `depsStorePath` for Go's no-subpath case. This is the highest-value test because the algorithm is now written once.
- **M1+M3 descriptor data:** in `ecosystems.test.ts`, assert each Ecosystem descriptor carries the right `sandbox` facet and each Package Manager descriptor carries the right `impureInstall`, so a half-added Ecosystem/manager is caught. Cheap, exhaustive-leaning coverage.
- **M4 `setupFor`/`envFor`:** extend/keep `plan.test.ts` so `planSandbox` still emits the same setup commands and env per Ecosystem and Package Manager (pure and impure paths) after the rewrite тАФ the behaviour-preserving guard.
- **Prior art:** `src/ecosystems/ecosystems.test.ts` (descriptor assertions) and `src/sandbox/plan.test.ts` (`planSandbox` setup/env assertions) are the existing patterns to follow.

## Out of Scope

- Adding any new Ecosystem or Package Manager (e.g. Rust) тАФ this PRD only relocates existing node/python/go + npm/pnpm/yarn/bun/pip/uv/poetry knowledge.
- Changing the actual staging commands, `cp` flags, env values, or impure-install commands. Any observable change to what the Sandbox runs is a regression, not a goal.
- The egress/proxy plumbing in `plan.ts` (`deriveEgress`, `proxyUrl`, mounts) тАФ untouched.
- Store provisioning, the Importers, impurity-signal reading, and pin-then-pure resolve тАФ already on the Registry; not revisited here.
- Any user-facing configuration of Ecosystems тАФ the Registry stays internally curated (ADR 0001).

## Further Notes

- This is explicitly *finishing a migration*, not inventing a pattern: five of six dispatch sites already derive from the Registry. The seam exists, has two adapters per facet (node/python/go for staging; multiple managers for impure install), and is already tested тАФ so it is a real seam, not a hypothetical one.
- The CONTEXT.md edit (Module 5) is small but matters: the glossary is the map future reviews and agents navigate by; leaving `sandbox` off the derive-list is part of what let this drift happen.
- Origin: surfaced via `/improve-codebase-architecture`; the prompting observation was the awkwardness of the three-way `if`-chain in `setupFor` and a desire to have it "declared by the ecosystem manager."
close_reason: All three child slices implemented and closed; sandbox now derives from the Ecosystem Registry. 474/474 unit tests pass.

title: Gated e2e: a real run() actually spawns the detached __autogc child and it frees
description:
## Gap

`test/e2e/gc-collect.test.ts` proves the GC ENGINE live (gated): it calls `autoGc()`
directly with an injected runner + forced size/disk, runs a real `optimise` тЖТ
conditional `--gc` on a scratch NP store, and asserts both a scoped-rooted and a
recency-rooted closure survive while unrooted paths are freed.

What it does NOT exercise is the **auto-FIRING from a real run**: the detached
`__autogc` child that `src/run/index.ts` spawns in the `withProvisionedSandbox`
`finally` (via `spawnAutoGc` тЖТ `node <cli> __autogc`, detached + unref). That hop is
only unit-tested with an injected spawner; `docs/v1-status.md` explicitly defers its
live proof to "the same gated capable-host path as the orchestration loop."

So the seam between "a run finished" and "the background sweep actually ran and freed"
has no end-to-end proof.

## What to build

A `DUSTCASTLE_E2E`-gated e2e (skipped without the flag; scratch `NP_LOCATION` under
tmpdir with the existing `$HOME`-guard) that:

- runs a real provisioned flow to completion (the smallest path that goes through the
  `withProvisionedSandbox` bracket тАФ likely a single `withProvisionedSandbox`/`run`
  over the node-sample fixture, reusing the warm store like the other gated e2e), with
  the auto-gc trigger forced over its ceiling (inject size/disk, or pre-fill the scratch
  store so it's genuinely over),
- waits for the **detached** child to finish (it is `detached + unref` тАФ poll `gc.log`
  / `gc.lock` with a bounded timeout rather than assuming synchronous completion),
- asserts the child actually ran: `gc.log` gained a sweep entry AND unrooted scratch
  paths were freed while THIS run's scoped + recency roots survived,
- optionally asserts the next run surfaces the `ЁЯз╣ тАж freed X` line (`readLastSweepLine`).

## Notes

- The detached/unref nature is the crux тАФ the test must not race the child. A bounded
  poll on the lock/log file is the honest wait.
- Keep it non-destructive to the warm store: dedicated scratch `NP_LOCATION`, same hard
  guard as `gc-collect.test.ts`.
- This closes the last unproven link in the auto-GC lifecycle (ADR 0007/0008): engine
  proven, persistence/roots proven, trigger brain unit-proven тАФ only the live spawn-from-
  run is unobserved.
acceptance_criteria: A DUSTCASTLE_E2E-gated e2e drives a real run()/orchestrate() (or withProvisionedSandbox) to completion and asserts the detached '__autogc' child was actually spawned AND ran: e.g. gc.log gained a sweep entry / a forced-over-ceiling scratch store had unrooted paths freed while the run's scoped + recency roots survived; the next run surfaces the 'ЁЯз╣ тАж freed X' line. Runs on a scratch NP_LOCATION (never $HOME), skipped without DUSTCASTLE_E2E.
